output: A committed JSON-LD contract definition, JSON Schema, and consumer-guide document, approved by the maintainer.
context: The fan-out contract is the typed interface between the rights-gate and downstream Elyos projects. It must be JSON-LD so the wider commons can ingest it, idempotent so re-runs never spawn duplicate downstream tasks, and must propagate attribution, jurisdiction, and license-basis. Depends on prov-001. See PLAN.md (Solution approach section 6).
title: Define + validate the cleared item JSON-LD fan-out contract (idempotent/dedup-keyed)
acceptanceCriteria: The contract is JSON-LD with vocabulary alignment to schema.org, Dublin Core, and IIIF where applicable.

Required fields include: item reference (LoC URL + item ID), media type(s), detected language(s), suggested downstream lane(s), provenance-manifest pointer, rights basis, jurisdiction, determination TTL expiry, and required courtesy attribution line.
The contract validates against a published JSON Schema committed to the repository.
Idempotency is guaranteed: keyed by LoC item ID + content hash so a re-run never emits a duplicate record.
CC-BY-SA share-alike obligations are recorded in the contract and carried into downstream intake.
The contract format is documented with a consumer guide explaining how to parse and use each field.
objective: Define and publish the cleared item JSON-LD fan-out contract aligned to schema.org/Dublin Core/IIIF, validated against a JSON Schema, idempotent and dedup-keyed by LoC item ID + content hash, carrying rights basis, jurisdiction, courtesy attribution, and suggested downstream lanes.
